title: Ricinocarpus zeyheri
intro: Ricinocarpus zeyheri, also known as the Cape hound's tongue or Zeyher's bushwillow, is a shrub or small tree native to Southern Africa, belonging to the family Combretaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory infections. Ricinocarpus zeyheri contains a range of bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and coumarins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, as well as in traditional medicine practices in Southern Africa.
